title: The geometry of the Chilean continental wedge: Tectonic segmentation of subduction processes off Chile
abstract: The study examines the precise continental wedge geometry along the Chilean margin using high-resolution bathymetry and geophysical data. Intricate patterns in wedge geometry challenge conventional classification approaches for convergent margin tectonics. The research demonstrates a latitudinal segmentation of the Chilean offshore subduction zone characterized by varying basal friction and fluid pressure conditions. Fluid pressure inside the continental wedge increases north of 33°S, attributed to fracturing from tectonic erosion. The findings correlate with coseismic rupture patterns of major Chilean megathrust earthquakes.

location.description: Full Chilean margin from ~18°S to 46°S, continental wedge geometry analysis based on compiled seismic and bathymetric data

data.name: Compiled bathymetric and seismic data — full Chilean margin
data.description: Compilation of published bathymetric data and seismic velocity models from published studies along the full Chilean margin (18°–46°S) used to characterize continental wedge geometry.

processing.summary: Compilation and analysis study using published high-resolution bathymetric data and published seismic velocity models to characterize the Chilean continental wedge geometry along the full margin (18°–46°S). No new seismic data acquired. Wedge taper angles, basal friction, and fluid pressure estimated from published data and critical wedge theory. Latitudinal segmentation correlated with megathrust earthquake rupture patterns.
workflow: 1. Compilation of published bathymetric surveys (GEBCO, ship multibeam)
2. Compilation of published seismic velocity models
4. Critical wedge analysis (taper angle, friction, fluid pressure)
processing.notes: Open access via Universidad de Chile repository (CC BY-NC-ND). Single-author paper by Maksymowicz (U. Chile). No new seismic data collected.

analysis_notes: Open access PDF available via Universidad de Chile institutional repository (CC BY-NC-ND). Abstract confirmed from Semantic Scholar. Compilation study with no new seismic data. Maksymowicz (Universidad de Chile) is sole author.
